explanations.7: The 'condition: service_healthy' means Docker won't start Joomla until the database container passes its health check (defined later with the mysqladmin ping test).
explanations.8: Setting 'required: true' ensures that if the database fails its health check, the entire compose file will fail to start instead of continuing anyway.
explanations.16: The 'null' value here means this service is connected to the joomla_network, but we're not setting any special network options for it.
explanations.23: The '1002:1002' sets which user ID and group ID run processes inside the container—this is a security practice to avoid running as root.
explanations.28: 'bind:' and 'create_host_path: true' tells Docker to automatically create the folder /tmp/joomla/joomla_data on your computer if it doesn't already exist.
explanations.35: The quoted '"'1'"' is a confusing way to set a boolean-like value to the string '1', which tells MySQL to generate a random root password instead of using a fixed one.
explanations.45: The 'retries: 10' means Docker will try the ping test up to 10 times before giving up, with 20-second timeouts, so the database has up to 200 seconds to become ready.
explanations.55: Just like line 23, this runs the database container as user ID 1002 instead of root for security.
explanations.61: Similar to line 28, this auto-creates /tmp/joomla/db_data on your computer to store the MySQL data persistently.
